solution.p: We will factor r^{2}-81 where a=1, b=0, and c=-81.

Difference of Squares Formula: The formula for a difference of squares is a^2-b^2 = (a+b)(a-b) Note that the polynomial r^{2}-81 matches the formula's right side, and we have: \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}-81\amp =\left(r\right)^2-\left(9\right)^2\newline \amp =\left(r+9\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-9\right)\end{aligned}
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.
For r^{2}-81, since the product of 9 and -9 is -81, and the sum of 9 and -9 is 0, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}-81\amp =\left(r+9\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-9\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (-81) = -81.

The number -81 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(-81)\amp -1(81)\amp 3(-27)\amp -3(27)
ewline9(-9)\amp -9(9)\amp \end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of 0, and this sum happens with (-9)+9=0 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]r^{2}-81\amp =r^{2}-9r+9r-81
ewline \amp =r\mathopen{}\left(r-9ight)+9\mathopen{}\left(r-9ight)
ewline \amp =\left(r+9ight)\mathopen{}\left(r-9ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in r^{2}-81 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}
The third term, -81, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(-81)\amp -1(81)\amp 3(-27)\amp -3(27)\newline9(-9)\amp -9(9)\amp \end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with r^{2}-81 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]r^{2}-81\amp =\left(r+9\right)\mathopen{}\left(r-9\right)\end{aligned}
